title: Lovely Moroccan Pepper Chebakia
description: This monsoon Moroccan dish features roasted pepper and tagine-cook in a low-sodium-friendly chebakia form, offering umami flavors and a cheese-pull experience.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the roasted red peppers, tagine spice mix, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and cook for 5 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked pepper mixture, crumbly feta cheese, and chopped parsley.
Layer the filo dough in a large baking dish, brushing each layer with the beaten eggs.
Spoon the pepper and cheese mixture onto the filo dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the filo dough over the filling to form a chebakia shape and brush the top with the remaining eggs.
Bake the chebakia in the preheated oven for 35 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove the chebakia from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
